seoTitle: How to Integrate ClickUp With Google Docs (2026)
metaDescription: Connect ClickUp to Google Docs so you can embed live task cards in your documents and keep project status updated without switching tabs.
intro: Keeping project tasks and team documentation in sync usually means constant tab-switching. This guide shows how to install the ClickUp add-on for Google Docs, authenticate your account, and embed live task cards directly inside your document text.

steps.heading: Open the add-ons marketplace
steps.body: In your Google Doc, click Extensions in the top menu, then select Get Add-ons to open the Google Workspace Marketplace.

steps.heading: Install the ClickUp integration
steps.body: Search for ClickUp, select the verified app listing, and click Install to add it to your Google Workspace.

steps.heading: Authorize your ClickUp account
steps.body: Review the requested permissions, click Allow, and sign in to your ClickUp workspace when redirected to complete the connection.

steps.heading: Embed a task inside your document
steps.body: Return to Google Docs, open the ClickUp panel from the sidebar, then paste a ClickUp task URL into the text to turn it into a live smart card.

steps.heading: Confirm the sync is working
steps.body: Change a task's status in ClickUp and check that the embedded card in your document reflects the update.

faq.question: Do I need a ClickUp account to use this integration?
faq.answer: Yes, you need an active ClickUp workspace account to authenticate and pull in task data.

faq.question: Will embedded task cards update automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, once linked, the smart card reflects real-time status changes made in ClickUp.

faq.question: Can I remove the integration later?
faq.answer: You can uninstall the add-on anytime from the Extensions menu without affecting your existing ClickUp tasks.

faq.question: Does this work with private ClickUp tasks?
faq.answer: You'll need appropriate permissions in ClickUp for a task to display correctly once embedded.
